short_description: CRITICAL P0: Production database primary node failure - COMPLETE SERVICE OUTAGE
description: At 2026-06-20T17:42:00Z, the PostgreSQL primary node (db-primary-prod-01, r6g.4xlarge) experienced a catastrophic storage subsystem failure. All write operations ceased immediately. Read replicas are serving stale data from approximately 17:41:58Z. The automatic failover to the standby node (db-standby-prod-01) DID NOT trigger because the replication lag exceeded the configured maximum of 30 seconds (actual lag: 47 seconds at time of failure). All production services dependent on the database are returning HTTP 503 errors. Affected services include: order-service, user-service, payment-gateway, inventory-service, notification-service, analytics-service, auth-gateway, and admin-portal. This is a COMPLETE service outage affecting 100% of customers globally (approximately 23,000 active sessions dropped). The incident was detected by automated health checks at 17:42:15Z and PagerDuty alerts were triggered for all on-call engineers. EBS volume status check shows 'impaired' status on the primary node's data volumes (vol-0a1b2c3d4e5f67890). AWS Health Dashboard does not show any regional issues. Current hypothesis: EBS volume corruption possibly due to an underlying hardware failure in the us-east-1c availability zone. Recovery options being evaluated: (1) Force promote standby to primary with potential data loss of ~47 seconds of transactions, (2) Attempt EBS volume recovery/snapshot restore on primary, (3) Point-in-time recovery from last automated backup (RPO: 15 minutes). Customer-facing status page has been updated to 'Major Outage'. Executive leadership and board members have been notified via the P0 escalation protocol.
business_impact: TOTAL SERVICE OUTAGE. 100% of customers affected globally. Approximately 23,000 active sessions terminated. All transactions halted. Estimated revenue impact: $120K+ per hour. Regulatory notification may be required if outage exceeds 4 hours (SOC 2 Type II commitment).
workaround: NONE. All database-dependent services are non-functional. Read replicas serving stale data only. Status page updated.
escalation_level: Executive - CTO and VP Engineering notified

timeline.event: Primary database node storage failure detected
PagerDuty alerts triggered for all on-call engineers
Automatic failover attempted but FAILED due to replication lag (47s > 30s threshold)
All production services confirmed returning 503 errors
Status page updated to Major Outage
P0 escalation protocol activated - executive leadership notified
War room established. DBA team evaluating forced failover vs backup restore
